categories.name: عَنَاصِرُ مِنْ قِصَّةِ مُوسَى وَالْخَضِر
categories.nameEn: Elements from the Story of Musa and Khidr

items.verse: فَلَمَّا بَلَغَا مَجْمَعَ بَيْنِهِمَا نَسِيَا حُوتَهُمَا فَٱتَّخَذَ سَبِيلَهُۥ فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ سَرَبًۭا
items.verseEn: But when they reached the junction between them, they forgot their fish, and it took its course into the sea, slipping away.

items.verse: أَمَّا ٱلسَّفِينَةُ فَكَانَتْ لِمَسَـٰكِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ فَأَرَدتُّ أَنْ أَعِيبَهَا وَكَانَ وَرَآءَهُم مَّلِكٌۭ يَأْخُذُ كُلَّ سَفِينَةٍ غَصْبًۭا
items.verseEn: As for the ship, it belonged to poor people working at sea. So I intended to cause defect in it as there was after them a king who seized every [good] ship by force.

items.verse: فَٱنطَلَقَا حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا لَقِيَا غُلَـٰمًۭا فَقَتَلَهُۥ قَالَ أَقَتَلْتَ نَفْسًۭا زَكِيَّةًۢ بِغَيْرِ نَفْسٍۢ لَّقَدْ جِئْتَ شَيْـًۭٔا نُّكْرًۭا
items.verseEn: So they set out, until when they met a boy, he [i.e., al-Khiḍr] killed him. [Moses] said, "Have you killed a pure soul for other than [having killed] a soul? You have certainly done a deplorable thing."

items.verse: قَالَ إِنَّكَ لَن تَسْتَطِيعَ مَعِىَ صَبْرًۭا
items.verseEn: He said, "Indeed, with me you will never be able to have patience.

categories.name: أَحْدَاثُ يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ فِي سُورَةِ الْمُرْسَلَاتِ
categories.nameEn: Events of the Day of Judgment in Surah Al-Mursalat

items.verseEn: So when the stars are obliterated
And when the heaven is opened
And when the mountains are blown away
And when the messengers' time has come...

items.verse: ۞ كُلُّ ٱلطَّعَامِ كَانَ حِلًّۭا لِّبَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ إِلَّا مَا حَرَّمَ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلُ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِهِۦ مِن قَبْلِ أَن تُنَزَّلَ ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةُ ۗ قُلْ فَأْتُوا۟ بِٱلتَّوْرَىٰةِ فَٱتْلُوهَآ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ
items.verseEn: All food was lawful to the Children of Israel except what Israel [i.e., Jacob] had made unlawful to himself before the Torah was revealed. Say, [O Muḥammad], "So bring the Torah and recite it, if you should be truthful."

items.verse: وَلْيَحْكُمْ أَهْلُ ٱلْإِنجِيلِ بِمَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ فِيهِ ۚ وَمَن لَّمْ يَحْكُم بِمَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْفَـٰسِقُونَ
items.verseEn: And let the People of the Gospel judge by what Allāh has revealed therein. And whoever does not judge by what Allāh has revealed - then it is those who are the defiantly disobedient.
Indeed, this is in the former scriptures,
And [by] the night when it covers [i.e., conceals] it
And [by] the day when it displays it

items.verse: فَيَوْمَئِذٍۢ لَّا يُسْـَٔلُ عَن ذَنۢبِهِۦٓ إِنسٌۭ وَلَا جَآنٌّۭ
items.verseEn: Then on that Day none will be asked about his sin among men or jinn.

items.verse: وَخَلَقَ ٱلْجَآنَّ مِن مَّارِجٍۢ مِّن نَّارٍۢ
items.verseEn: And He created the jinn from a smokeless flame of fire.

harf.hint: A divinely prescribed path or law; often translated as 'law'
harf.arabicVerse: ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَـٰكَ عَلَىٰ شَرِيعَةٍۢ مِّنَ ٱلْأَمْرِ فَٱتَّبِعْهَا وَلَا تَتَّبِعْ أَهْوَآءَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
harf.verse: 45:18 - Then We put you, [O Muḥammad], on an ordained way concerning the matter [of religion]; so follow it and do not follow the inclinations of those who do not know.

deduction.title: The Army That Was Met by Flying Clay
deduction.intro: In the era before Prophet Muhammad, a powerful ruler from Yemen launched a military campaign aimed at destroying a sacred sanctuary in Makkah. This enemy led a vast army complete with war elephants, confident in their strength and numbers. However, their journey would end abruptly as they encountered an unexpected divine intervention. Can you identify who faced this remarkable reckoning?
clues: We marched with a massive army and powerful war elephants toward the sacred house
We intended to uproot and destroy a place of worship built by servants of the Most Merciful
A flock of small birds was sent against us, carrying clay stones in their beaks and claws
We were met with a sound that threw stones upon us, turning us into trampled remnants
I was from the land of Yemen and served under a ruler who sought to挑衅 the sacred sanctuary
We were made an example of for those who would dare to desafío the sanctuary of Allah

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: A siege of a desert fortress
An invasion of the sacred sanctuary in Makkah
A rebellion against a tyrant king
A search for a lost prophet
trial.answer: An invasion of the sacred sanctuary in Makkah

location.label: Key Element
options: The valley of Mina near Makkah
The palace of Sana'a in Yemen
The shores of the Red Sea
location.answer: The valley of Mina near Makkah

outcome.label: Outcome
options: Victory and destruction of the sacred house
Defeat by birds carrying clay stones
outcome.answer: Defeat by birds carrying clay stones

deduction.verse: Did He not make their plan into misguidance? (105:2-4)

scramble.hint: A verse describing what happens to the mighty mountains on the Day of Resurrection.
scramble.verseEn: And at the earth - how it is spread out?
